title: Ootukulangara Bhagavathy Temple, Peruvemba
tagline: Ootukulangara Bhagavathy Temple, Peruvemba — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Oottukulangara Bhagavathy temple is the Hindu temple situated in Peruvemba village of Palakkad district in the state of Kerala.
overview.description: Oottukulangara Bhagavathy temple is the Hindu temple situated in Peruvemba village of Palakkad district in the state of Kerala. The primary deity of the temple is Sri Ootukulangara Bhagavathy along with Lord Ganapathy and Lord Ayyappan. The temple timings are from 5 AM to 10.30 AM and 5 PM to 7 PM. Main offerings for the Ootukulangara Bhagavathy are Chanthatam, Kadumadura Payasam, Villaku, Trikaala pooja and Udayasthamana Pooja along with various other offerings. Chanthatam can be performed only in the morning pooja section of the temple.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

topPlaces.name: Peruvemba
topPlaces.description: Peruvemba is a village and gram panchayat near Chittur-Thathamangalam in the Palakkad district of Kerala, India. Within the village is the Ootukulangara Bhagavathy Temple.

topPlaces.name: Kinassery, Palakkad
topPlaces.description: Kinassery is a village in Palakkad district of Kerala state, India. It is close by the village of Koduvayur.

topPlaces.name: Kodumbu Subramanya Swamy Temple
topPlaces.description: Kodumbu Subramaniya Swamy Temple is one of the oldest Murugan temples in Kerala region. It is located in the village of Kodumba in Palakkad district, 10 kilometres (6.2 mi) southeast of Palakkad and southwest of Coimbatore in the valley of the Soka Nasini river, Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Puthunagaram
topPlaces.description: Puthunagaram is a town and gram panchayat in the Palakkad district, state of Kerala, India. It is about 10 km south of Palakkad and 8 Km from Kollengode.

topPlaces.name: Polpully
topPlaces.description: Polpully is a village and gram panchayat in Palakkad district in the state of Kerala, India. The village is located 10 km (6.2 mi) from Palakkad city, and 5 km (3.1 mi) from Chittur.

topPlaces.name: Chittur taluk
topPlaces.description: Chittur is one among the 6 taluks in Palakkad district of Indian state of Kerala. There are 30 revenue villages in chittur taluk.

topPlaces.name: Chittur-Thathamangalam
topPlaces.description: Chittur-Thathamangalam is a town and municipality in the Palakkad district of Kerala State, India. It is the headquarters of Chittur taluk, 13 km (8.1 mi) southeast of Palakkad, on the banks of the Kannadipuzha, a major headstream of the Bharathapuzha, the second-longest river in Kerala.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Ootukulangara Bhagavathy Temple, Peruvemba?
faq.a: The best time to visit Ootukulangara Bhagavathy Temple, Peruvemba is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Ootukulangara Bhagavathy Temple, Peruvemba?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Coimbatore International Airport (53 km) and the nearest railway station is Coimbatore Junction (45 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Ootukulangara Bhagavathy Temple, Peruvemba?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Ootukulangara Bhagavathy Temple, Peruvemba at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Ootukulangara Bhagavathy Temple, Peruvemba?
faq.a: Stays in Ootukulangara Bhagavathy Temple, Peruvemba start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Ootukulangara Bhagavathy Temple, Peruvemba?
faq.a: The top attractions are Peruvemba, Kodumba, Kinassery, Palakkad, Kodumbu Subramanya Swamy Temple, Puthunagaram.
